rulings.Case Title: BORIS LANDAU VS. REASSURE AMERICA LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 2, 2011, Line 5. DEFENDANT DEBORAH CAHN-WEINER, PH.D'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the fourth cause of action and sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the fifth and sixth causes of action. Plaintiff has sufficiently pled an intentional infliction of emotional distress claim (See Younan v. Equifax Inc. (1980) 111 Cal.App.3d 498). Plaintiff is given leave to amend her interference torts to allege that Dr. Cahn-Weiner was a ¿stranger¿ and not an ¿agent¿ insofar as the alleged tortuous conduct is concerned. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BORIS LANDAU VS. REASSURE AMERICA LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 2, 2011, Line 5. DEFENDANT REASSURE AMERICA LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the first cause of action and sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the third cause of action. Defendant lacks standing to demurrer to a claim alleged against another party. As to the third cause of action for intentional misrepresentation, plaintiff is given leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege ultimate facts showing that his reliance on defendants¿ representations about the medical examiner¿s ¿independent¿ status was a substantial factor in causing plaintiff¿s alleged harm. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PHILIP E. KAY, VS. STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 2, 2011, Line 10. DEFENDANT HELENE WASSERMAN AND RALPHS GROCERY COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend as to the ninth and tenth cause of action and sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the eleventh and twelfth causes of action. As to the ninth and tenth causes of action, there is no private right of action under the statutes claimed to be the bases for those claims and the alleged misconduct is privileged. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege a short statement of non-time-barred wrongful conduct committed by the demurring defendants that violated plaintiff¿s rights under section 1983 and and/or a a short statement of non-time barred conduct committed by others as to which the demurring defendants engaged in a conspiracy that violated plaintiff¿s section 1983 rights. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)
